rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 13, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD (CALTRAIN), NATIONAL RAILROAD PASSENGER CORPORATION (AMTRAK), PENINSULA CORRIDER JOINT POWERS BOARD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar. The City cross-complaint was dismissed on May 29, 2012. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 13, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD (CALTRAIN), NATIONAL RAILROAD PASSENGER CORPORATION (AMTRAK) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied in its entirety. NRPC did not satisfy its initial summary judgment/ adjudication burden. The evidence cited at undisputed material fact 13 fails to demonstrate that NRPC did not create the pothole. NRPC does not present affirmative evidence or cite germane factually devoid discovery to support that assertion. The Wong and Prather depositions establish that potholes can occur quickly; they do not show that NRPC was not the proximate cause for the hole. Nor has NRPC shown that plaintiff is unable to establish liability against NRPC for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL CHARLTON VS. CARL ERNST JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Carl Ernest Jr'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories; Special Interrogatories; And Requests For Production Of Documents; And To Compel Production Of Documents; And For An Order That All Objections Are Waived; And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2215
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 13, 2012, LINE 3, DEFENDANT CARL ERNEST JR'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, FOR AN ORDER THAT ALL OBJECTIONS ARE WAIVED AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2215. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM F RYDER VS. SATEL LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 13, 2012, Line 9. CROSS DEFENDANT WILLIAM RYDER DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. The first cross- claim fails to allege sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action for fraud against Mr. William Ryder. That claim does not allege with sufficient particularity an actionable intentional misrepresentation by Mr. William Ryder as to which cross-complainants justifiably relied to their detriment. It does not appear likely that cross-complainants can cure this deficiency. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 13, 2012, Line 16. DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE UNIVERSITY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety.
